editionTitle: IB ACIO Daily Current Affairs — 19 July 2026
editionTitle_hi: आईबी एसीआईओ दैनिक करेंट अफेयर्स — 19 जुलाई 2026

articles.title: Kargil Vijay Diwas — 26 July (Exam Preview)
articles.summary: Kargil Vijay Diwas (26 July) commemorates India’s victory in the 1999 Kargil War and honours the Armed Forces’ sacrifice.
articles.summary_hi: कारगिल विजय दिवस (26 जुलाई) 1999 कारगिल युद्ध में भारत की विजय और सशस्त्र बलों के बलिदान का स्मरण।
articles.content:
Kargil Vijay Diwas is observed every year on 26 July to mark the successful conclusion of Operation Vijay in the Kargil War of 1999.

Pakistani intruders and soldiers occupied high positions in the Kargil sector of Ladakh (then J&K). The Indian Army and Air Force (Operation Safed Sagar) evicted them after intense high-altitude combat.

Heroes include Capt. Vikram Batra, Capt. Manoj Pandey, Rifleman Sanjay Kumar, and others awarded Param Vir Chakra. The war underscored the need for surveillance and border vigilance.

For IB ACIO: date (26 July), year (1999), Operation Vijay, and Operation Safed Sagar (IAF) are must-know facts.
articles.content_hi:
26 जुलाई — Operation Vijay (1999) की सफलता।

IAF: Operation Safed Sagar। Param Vir Chakra वीर।

IB ACIO: 26 July, 1999, Operation Vijay — याद रखें।
articles.question: Kargil Vijay Diwas is celebrated on which date?
articles.question_hi: कारगिल विजय दिवस किस तिथि को मनाया जाता है?
articles.explanation: Kargil Vijay Diwas is observed on 26 July every year.
articles.explanation_hi: कारगिल विजय दिवस हर वर्ष 26 जुलाई को मनाया जाता है।
articles.moreInfo: Related upcoming: National Flag Adoption Day (22 July). Vijay Diwas (16 Dec) marks 1971 war victory.
articles.moreInfo_hi: आगामी: ध्वज दिवस 22 जुलाई। 16 दिसंबर = 1971 विजय दिवस।

articles.title: National Flag Adoption Day — 22 July (Preview)
articles.summary: On 22 July 1947, the Constituent Assembly adopted the National Flag of India in its present form — tricolour with Ashoka Chakra.
articles.summary_hi: 22 जुलाई 1947 को संविधान सभा ने वर्तमान रूप में राष्ट्रीय ध्वज अपनाया — तिरंगा व अशोक चक्र।
articles.content:
The National Flag of India was adopted by the Constituent Assembly on 22 July 1947. It is a horizontal tricolour of deep saffron, white, and India green, with the navy-blue Ashoka Chakra (24 spokes) in the centre.

The Flag Code of India, 2002 governs display and respect for the flag. The Prevention of Insults to National Honour Act, 1971 penalises insult to the flag and anthem.

Pingali Venkayya is credited with the design of the Indian National Flag (with later modifications including the Chakra replacing the charkha).

For IB ACIO: date (22 July 1947), 24 spokes, Flag Code 2002, and Pingali Venkayya.
articles.content_hi:
22 जुलाई 1947 — संविधान सभा ने ध्वज अपनाया। केसरिया, सफेद, हरा + 24 तीलियों वाला अशोक चक्र।

Flag Code 2002। Pingali Venkayya डिज़ाइन।

IB ACIO: 22 July 1947, 24 spokes — याद रखें।
articles.question: India’s National Flag was adopted by the Constituent Assembly on:
articles.question_hi: भारत का राष्ट्रीय ध्वज संविधान सभा ने कब अपनाया?
articles.explanation: The Constituent Assembly adopted the National Flag on 22 July 1947.
articles.explanation_hi: संविधान सभा ने 22 जुलाई 1947 को राष्ट्रीय ध्वज अपनाया।
articles.moreInfo: Constitution was adopted 26 Nov 1949; Republic Day 26 Jan 1950.
articles.moreInfo_hi: संविधान अंगीकरण 26 नवंबर 1949; गणतंत्र दिवस 26 जनवरी 1950।

articles.title: Svamitva Yojana — Property Cards for Rural India
articles.title_hi: स्वामित्व योजना — ग्रामीण भारत के लिए संपत्ति कार्ड
articles.summary: SVAMITVA uses drone technology to map rural inhabited areas and issue property cards, enabling clear ownership and credit access.
articles.summary_hi: SVAMITVA ड्रोन से ग्रामीण आबादी क्षेत्रों का मानचित्रण और संपत्ति कार्ड — स्पष्ट स्वामित्व व ऋण पहुंच।
articles.content:
SVAMITVA (Survey of Villages and Mapping with Improvised Technology in Village Areas) was launched on National Panchayati Raj Day (24 April 2020/21 rollout).

It is implemented by the Ministry of Panchayati Raj with Survey of India using drones for large-scale mapping of rural abadi areas. Property cards (Gharaunis) help villagers monetise property as a financial asset.

The scheme reduces land disputes, improves Gram Panchayat tax collection, and supports planning.

For IB ACIO: drone mapping, Ministry of Panchayati Raj, and property cards for rural households.
articles.content_hi:
SVAMITVA: ड्रोन मैपिंग, पंचायती राज मंत्रालय, Survey of India।

संपत्ति कार्ड — विवाद कम, ऋण आसान।

IB ACIO: drone survey, Panchayati Raj ministry — याद रखें।
articles.question: SVAMITVA Yojana is primarily implemented by which Ministry?
articles.question_hi: स्वामित्व योजना मुख्यतः किस मंत्रालय द्वारा लागू होती है?
articles.explanation: SVAMITVA is implemented by the Ministry of Panchayati Raj with Survey of India.
articles.explanation_hi: SVAMITVA पंचायती राज मंत्रालय द्वारा Survey of India के साथ लागू।
articles.moreInfo: National Panchayati Raj Day is 24 April (73rd Amendment related).

articles.title: GST Council — Cooperative Federalism in Taxation
articles.title_hi: GST परिषद — कराधान में सहकारी संघवाद
articles.summary: The GST Council is a constitutional body under Article 279A that recommends GST rates, exemptions, and related tax reforms.
articles.summary_hi: GST परिषद धारा 279A के तहत संवैधानिक निकाय — GST दरें, छूट और कर सुधार सिफारिश।
articles.content:
The GST Council was constituted under Article 279A (inserted by the 101st Constitutional Amendment). The Union Finance Minister is Chairperson; state finance ministers are members.

It recommends tax rates, exemptions, threshold limits, and special provisions for states. Decisions aim at consensus; voting formula gives Centre one-third weight and States two-thirds.

GST subsumed multiple central and state indirect taxes from 1 July 2017. 1 July is also observed as GST Day in India.

For IB ACIO: Article 279A, 101st Amendment, FM as Chairperson, and GST from 1 July 2017.
articles.content_hi:
GST परिषद धारा 279A, 101वां संशोधन। वित्त मंत्री अध्यक्ष।

GST 1 जुलाई 2017 से। मतभार: केंद्र 1/3, राज्य 2/3।

IB ACIO: 279A, 101st Amendment — याद रखें।
articles.question: GST Council is provided under which Article of the Constitution?
articles.question_hi: GST परिषद संविधान की किस धारा के तहत है?
articles.explanation: Article 279A provides for the Goods and Services Tax Council.
articles.explanation_hi: धारा 279A GST परिषद का प्रावधान करती है।
articles.moreInfo: Related: GST reform overview (10 July edition).

articles.title: CDS and Department of Military Affairs
articles.title_hi: CDS और सैन्य मामलों का विभाग
articles.summary: India created the post of Chief of Defence Staff (CDS) in 2019–20 to improve tri-service synergy; DMA was set up under the MoD.
articles.summary_hi: भारत ने 2019–20 में CDS पद सृजित किया — त्रि-सेवा समन्वय; MoD के तहत DMA।
articles.content:
The Chief of Defence Staff (CDS) is the permanent chairman of the Chiefs of Staff Committee and the principal military adviser to the Defence Minister on tri-service matters.

The post was created after the recommendation of the Kargil Review Committee and later reforms; Gen. Bipin Rawat was the first CDS (2020). The Department of Military Affairs (DMA) was established under the Ministry of Defence, headed by the CDS.

CDS drives theatre commands, jointness in procurement, and integration of the Army, Navy, and Air Force.

For IB ACIO: first CDS (Gen. Bipin Rawat), DMA under MoD, and link to theatre commands.
articles.content_hi:
CDS: त्रि-सेवा सलाहकार, Chiefs of Staff Committee के स्थायी अध्यक्ष।

प्रथम CDS: Gen. Bipin Rawat (2020)। DMA — रक्षा मंत्रालय।

IB ACIO: CDS, DMA, theatre commands — याद रखें।
articles.question: Who was appointed as India’s first Chief of Defence Staff?
articles.question_hi: भारत के प्रथम Chief of Defence Staff कौन नियुक्त हुए?
articles.explanation: General Bipin Rawat was appointed as India’s first CDS in 2020.
articles.explanation_hi: जनरल बिपिन रावत 2020 में भारत के प्रथम CDS बने।
articles.moreInfo: Related: Integrated Theatre Commands (12 July edition).

articles.title: India’s SemiConductor Mission — India Semiconductor Mission
articles.title_hi: भारत का सेमीकंडक्टर मिशन — India Semiconductor Mission
articles.summary: India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) aims to build a domestic chip ecosystem with fabrication, packaging, and design incentives.
articles.summary_hi: India Semiconductor Mission घरेलू चिप पारिस्थितिकी — fabrication, packaging, design प्रोत्साहन।
articles.content:
The India Semiconductor Mission was launched under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) as part of the broader Semicon India programme.

It offers fiscal support for setting up semiconductor fabs, display fabs, compound semiconductors, and ATMP/OSAT packaging units. Design-linked Incentive (DLI) scheme supports chip design startups.

Strategic goal: reduce import dependence, secure supply chains, and create high-tech jobs. Related: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) for electronics manufacturing.

For IB ACIO: MeitY, ISM/Semicon India, and DLI/PLI link are technology policy facts.
articles.content_hi:
ISM: MeitY, Semicon India। fab, packaging, DLI design प्रोत्साहन।

PLI इलेक्ट्रॉनिक्स से जुड़ा। आयात निर्भरता कम करना लक्ष्य।

IB ACIO: MeitY, Semicon India — याद रखें।
articles.question: India Semiconductor Mission functions under which Ministry?
articles.question_hi: India Semiconductor Mission किस मंत्रालय के तहत काम करता है?
articles.explanation: ISM is under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY).
articles.explanation_hi: ISM इलेक्ट्रॉनिकी और सूचना प्रौद्योगिकी मंत्रालय (MeitY) के तहत है।
articles.moreInfo: Related: Digital India (13 July), UPI/Aadhaar digital stack editions.
articles.moreInfo_hi: संबंधित: Digital India, UPI, Aadhaar संस्करण।

articles.title: BIMSTEC — Bay of Bengal Regional Cooperation
articles.title_hi: BIMSTEC — बंगाल की खाड़ी क्षेत्रीय सहयोग
articles.summary: BIMSTEC links South and Southeast Asia around the Bay of Bengal — seven members including India, Thailand, and Bangladesh.
articles.summary_hi: BIMSTEC दक्षिण और दक्षिण-पूर्व एशिया को Bay of Bengal से जोड़ता है — 7 सदस्य incl. India, Thailand, Bangladesh।
articles.content:
BIMSTEC (Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation) has seven members: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Thailand.

It bridges SAARC and ASEAN geographies. Priority sectors include security, connectivity, trade, energy, and disaster management. The BIMSTEC Secretariat is in Dhaka, Bangladesh.

India sees BIMSTEC as key to its ‘Neighbourhood First’ and Act East policies, especially after SAARC stagnation.

For IB ACIO: 7 members, Secretariat (Dhaka), and Bay of Bengal focus.
articles.content_hi:
BIMSTEC 7 सदस्य: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Thailand।

Secretariat: Dhaka। Neighbourhood First + Act East।

IB ACIO: 7 सदस्य, Dhaka — याद रखें।
articles.question: Where is the BIMSTEC Secretariat located?
articles.explanation: The BIMSTEC Secretariat is located in Dhaka, Bangladesh.
articles.explanation_hi: BIMSTEC सचिवालय ढाका, बांग्लादेश में है।
articles.moreInfo: Compare: SAARC HQ Kathmandu; ASEAN HQ Jakarta; SCO HQ Beijing.
articles.moreInfo_hi: तुलना: SAARC Kathmandu; ASEAN Jakarta; SCO Beijing।

articles.title: Wetlands and Ramsar Sites in India
articles.title_hi: आर्द्रभूमि और भारत के रामसर स्थल
articles.summary: India is a party to the Ramsar Convention (1971). Ramsar sites are wetlands of international importance; India has one of the largest networks in Asia.
articles.summary_hi: भारत Ramsar Convention (1971) का पक्षकार। Ramsar स्थल अंतर्राष्ट्रीय महत्व की आर्द्रभूमियां।
articles.content:
The Ramsar Convention on Wetlands was signed in 1971 at Ramsar, Iran. It provides a framework for conservation and wise use of wetlands.

India joined in 1982. Chilika Lake (Odisha) and Keoladeo National Park (Rajasthan) were among early Indian Ramsar sites. World Wetlands Day is observed on 2 February.

Wetlands support biodiversity, groundwater recharge, and flood control. India’s Ramsar count has expanded significantly in recent years across states.

For IB ACIO: Ramsar 1971 (Iran), India joined 1982, World Wetlands Day 2 February.
articles.content_hi:
Ramsar Convention 1971, Iran। भारत 1982 में शामिल।

Chilika, Keoladeo प्रारंभिक स्थल। World Wetlands Day: 2 फरवरी।

IB ACIO: 1971, 1982, 2 Feb — याद रखें।
articles.question: The Ramsar Convention on Wetlands was adopted in which year?
articles.question_hi: आर्द्रभूमियों पर Ramsar Convention किस वर्ष अपनाया गया?
articles.explanation: The Ramsar Convention was adopted in 1971 at Ramsar, Iran.
articles.explanation_hi: Ramsar Convention 1971 में Ramsar, Iran में अपनाया गया।

articles.title: Sixth Schedule — Autonomous Councils in Northeast
articles.title_hi: छठी अनुसूची — पूर्वोत्तर में स्वायत्त परिषदें
articles.summary: The Sixth Schedule provides for Autonomous District Councils in Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram to protect tribal self-governance.
articles.summary_hi: छठी अनुसूची Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, Mizoram में स्वायत्त जिला परिषदें — जनजातीय स्वशासन।
articles.content:
The Sixth Schedule to the Constitution contains special provisions for the administration of tribal areas in Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram.

Autonomous District Councils (ADCs) can make laws on land, forest, shifting cultivation, village administration, and inheritance (subject to the Governor/State). This protects customary tribal rights.

Contrast: Fifth Schedule deals with Scheduled Areas and Tribes in other states; PESA Act applies to Fifth Schedule areas.

For IB ACIO: four states under Sixth Schedule, ADC powers, and Fifth vs Sixth Schedule difference.
articles.content_hi:
छठी अनुसूची: Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, Mizoram। Autonomous District Councils।

पांचवीं अनुसूची: अन्य राज्यों के Scheduled Areas। PESA पांचवीं से जुड़ा।

IB ACIO: 4 राज्य, ADC — याद रखें।
articles.question: Sixth Schedule Autonomous Councils apply to which of these states?
articles.question_hi: छठी अनुसूची की स्वायत्त परिषदें इनमें से किन राज्यों पर लागू?
articles.explanation: Sixth Schedule covers tribal areas of Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram.
articles.explanation_hi: छठी अनुसूची Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura और Mizoram के जनजातीय क्षेत्रों पर लागू।
articles.moreInfo: Related: Article 370/J&K reorganisation (10 July) as another special-status topic.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup 2022 — Key Facts for Exams
articles.title_hi: फीफा विश्व कप 2022 — परीक्षा के लिए मुख्य तथ्य
articles.summary: Qatar hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup — first in the Middle East. Argentina won; Lionel Messi earned his first World Cup title.
articles.summary_hi: कतर ने 2022 FIFA World Cup की मेज़बानी — मध्य पूर्व में पहला। Argentina विजेता; Messi प्रथम विश्व कप।
articles.content:
The 22nd FIFA World Cup was held in Qatar in 2022 — the first World Cup in the Arab world and played in winter (Nov–Dec) due to heat.

Argentina defeated France in the final on penalties; Lionel Messi won the Golden Ball. Kylian Mbappé scored a hat-trick in the final. Morocco became the first African team to reach a World Cup semi-final.

FIFA headquarters is in Zurich, Switzerland. The next men’s World Cups are scheduled on a four-year cycle (2026 co-hosted by USA, Canada, Mexico).

For IB ACIO: Qatar 2022, Argentina champions, and 2026 hosts (USA–Canada–Mexico).
articles.content_hi:
FIFA WC 2022: Qatar। विजेता Argentina। Messi Golden Ball।

2026: USA, Canada, Mexico सह-मेज़बान। FIFA HQ: Zurich।

IB ACIO: Qatar 2022, Argentina — याद रखें।
articles.question: Which country hosted the FIFA World Cup 2022?
articles.question_hi: FIFA विश्व कप 2022 की मेज़बानी किस देश ने की?
articles.explanation: Qatar hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup — the first in the Middle East.
articles.explanation_hi: कतर ने 2022 FIFA विश्व कप की मेज़बानी की — मध्य पूर्व में पहला।
articles.moreInfo: Related: Cricket World Cup 2023 India host (13 July edition).
